pi_items.source: Verified from local stations.txt that 25544 is ISS (ZARYA) and from Wikipedia that 20580 is the Hubble Space Telescope.
Prompt explicitly asks for New York studio at 40.7128 N.
Prompt explicitly asks for New York studio at 74.0060 W, which translates to -74.0060 longitude.
Prompt explicitly asks to 'turn OFF the map grid lines'.
Prompt explicitly asks to 'turn OFF the sun and moon icons'.
Prompt explicitly asks to 'turn OFF the sun and moon icons'.
Prompt explicitly asks to 'turn ON the day/night shadow'.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real orbital TLE data. It has been verified that NORAD ID 25544 corresponds to the ISS (ZARYA) and 20580 corresponds to the Hubble Space Telescope. The target New York ground station coordinates from the prompt exactly match the latitude 40.7128 and longitude -74.0060 at 10m altitude. The required GPredict UI preference modifications (disabling grid, sun, moon, and enabling shadow) perfectly align with the expected boolean values.
